title: Introduction
content:
Introduction
FastBackend is a schema-driven backend runtime that transforms database schemas into dynamic REST APIs with OpenAPI specifications.
It mirrors UIGen's architecture: routes exist only in memory at runtime. Only IR and OpenAPI are written to disk.
What FastBackend Does
What You Own
Packages
- Schema parsing, IR generation, OpenAPI generation
- Command-line interface
- Python FastAPI runtime adapter
Examples
SQLAlchemy + FastAPI
Prisma + FastAPI

title: How It Works
content:
How It Works
FastBackend follows a three-stage pipeline:
Stage 1: Schema Parsing
The CLI reads your schema file and parses entities, fields, relationships, and enums.
Supported formats:
SQLAlchemy ()
Prisma ()
Stage 2: IR Generation
The parser output is normalized into a versioned Intermediate Representation (IR) at .
OpenAPI is generated from the IR at .
No route code is generated. FastBackend does not write CRUD routers to disk.
Stage 3: Runtime
The FastAPI adapter reads IR at startup and dynamically registers:
CRUD routes per entity
Relationship routes
Custom endpoints from 
Health check at 
Pydantic models are created at runtime for request validation.
Watch Mode
When  is enabled in , the CLI watches your schema and config files and re-runs  on change. Uvicorn hot-reload picks up Python changes in custom endpoints.

content:

title: Runtime Architecture
content:
Runtime Architecture
The  package reads IR and builds the application at startup.
Engines
Route Registry
The runtime tracks which routes were registered and which were overridden by custom code. Overridden paths are skipped during CRUD registration.
Custom Endpoints
Files in  export a FastAPI . The runtime imports each module and calls .
Entry Point
Defaults:
IR path: 
Custom path: 
In-Memory Store
The MVP runtime uses an in-memory store for CRUD operations during development. Production deployments should connect engines to your database layer as adapters evolve.

title: build

fastbackend build
Generate production IR and OpenAPI (same as  for the MVP).
Use in CI before deploying. Ensure  is available to the runtime in your deployment environment.

content:
fastbackend test
Run framework-specific tests for the current project.
Options
Example
Scaffolded projects include , , and .

title: migrate
content:
fastbackend migrate
Run database migrations. Adapter-specific behavior applies.
For SQLAlchemy projects, integrate Alembic in your project. For Prisma, use . FastBackend does not replace your migration tool.

title: docker:build
content:
fastbackend docker:build
Build a Docker image using the project Dockerfile.
Options
Example
Run  to scaffold Dockerfile templates. Use  in the Dockerfile CMD for multi-stage builds.

See examples/sqlalchemy-fastapi/app/custom/ for working examples.
